sourceQuestion:
To pass a test, a candidate needs to answer at least 2 out of 3 questions correctly. A total of 6,30,000 candidates appeared for the test. Question A was answered correctly by 3,30,000 candidates. Question B was answered correctly by 2,50,000 candidates. Question C was answered correctly by 2,60,000 candidates. Both questions A and B were answered correctly by 1,00,000 candidates. Both questions B and C were answered correctly by 90,000 candidates. Both questions A and C were answered correctly by 80,000 candidates. If the number of students answering all 3 questions correctly is 60,000, how many candidates failed the test?

A. 4,20,000
B. 2,10,000
C. 3,90,000
D. 4,20,000

parsed.stem: To pass a test, a candidate needs to answer at least 2 out of 3 questions correctly. A total of 6,30,000 candidates appeared for the test. Question A was answered correctly by 3,30,000 candidates. Question B was answered correctly by 2,50,000 candidates. Question C was answered correctly by 2,60,000 candidates. Both questions A and B were answered correctly by 1,00,000 candidates. Both questions B and C were answered correctly by 90,000 candidates. Both questions A and C were answered correctly by 80,000 candidates. If the number of students answering all 3 questions correctly is 60,000, how many candidates failed the test?

sourceQuestion:
An e-mail password must contain three characters. The password has to contain one numeral from 0 to 9, one upper case and one lower case character from the English alphabet. How many distinct passwords are possible?

A. 6,760
B. 13,520
C. 40,560
D. 1,05,456

parsed.stem: An e-mail password must contain three characters. The password has to contain one numeral from 0 to 9, one upper case and one lower case character from the English alphabet. How many distinct passwords are possible?

sourceQuestion:
A cube is built using 64 cubic blocks of side one unit. After it is built, one cubic block is removed from every corner of the cube. The resulting surface area of the body (in square units) after the removal is ________.

A. 56  B. 64  C. 72  D. 96

parsed.stem: A cube is built using 64 cubic blocks of side one unit. After it is built, one cubic block is removed from every corner of the cube. The resulting surface area of the body (in square units) after the removal is ________.

sourceQuestion:
If theta is the angle, in degrees, between the longest diagonal of the cube and any one of the edges of the cube, then, cos(theta) = ?

A. 1/2  B. 1/sqrt(3)  C. 1/sqrt(2)  D. sqrt(3)/2

parsed.stem: If theta is the angle, in degrees, between the longest diagonal of the cube and any one of the edges of the cube, then, cos(theta) = ?

sourceQuestion:
Raju has 24 currency notes in his pocket consisting of only Rs. 20 notes and Rs. 10 notes. The total money value of the notes is Rs. 230. The number of Rs. 10 notes that Raju has is:

A. 5  B. 6  C. 9  D. 10

parsed.stem: Raju has 24 currency notes in his pocket consisting of only Rs. 20 notes and Rs. 10 notes. The total money value of the notes is Rs. 230. The number of Rs. 10 notes that Raju has is:

sourceQuestion:
In the given diagram, ovals are marked at different heights (h) of a hill. Which one of the following options P, Q, R, and S depicts the top view of the hill?

A. P  B. Q  C. R  D. S

parsed.stem: In the given diagram, ovals are marked at different heights (h) of a hill. Which one of the following options P, Q, R, and S depicts the top view of the hill?

sourceQuestion:
Consider a simple connected graph G with n vertices and n+1 edges (n > 2). Then, which of the following statements are true?
A. G has no cycles
B. The graph obtained by removing any edge from G is not connected
C. G has at least one cycle
D. The graph obtained by removing any two edges from G is not connected
E. None of the above

parsed.stem: Consider a simple connected graph G with n vertices and n+1 edges (n > 2). Then, which of the following statements are true?

options.text: The graph obtained by removing any edge from G is not connected
G has at least one cycle
The graph obtained by removing any two edges from G is not connected

sourceQuestion:
The number of distinct simple graphs with up to three nodes is:
A. 15
B. 10
C. 7
D. 9

parsed.stem: The number of distinct simple graphs with up to three nodes is:

sourceQuestion:
Select the graph that schematically represents BOTH y = x^m and y = x^(1/m) properly in the interval 0 <= x <= 1, for integer values of m, where m > 1.

A. [Graph A: y = x^(1/m) is the upper curve (bows upward) and y = x^m is the lower curve (bows downward), both passing through (0,0) and (1,1)]
B. [Graph B: y = x^m is the upper curve and y = x^(1/m) is the lower curve]
C. [Graph C: y = x^m is the upper curve and y = x^(1/m) is the lower curve, curves close together]
D. [Graph D: y = x^(1/m) is the upper curve but both curves appear concave toward the origin]
sourceAnswer: A. y = x^(1/m) above y = x^m on (0,1)

parsed.stem: Select the graph that schematically represents BOTH y = x^m and y = x^(1/m) properly in the interval 0 <= x <= 1, for integer values of m, where m > 1.

options.text: [Graph A: y = x^(1/m) is the upper curve (bows upward) and y = x^m is the lower curve (bows downward), both passing through (0,0) and (1,1)]
[Graph B: y = x^m is the upper curve and y = x^(1/m) is the lower curve]
[Graph C: y = x^m is the upper curve and y = x^(1/m) is the lower curve, curves close together]
[Graph D: y = x^(1/m) is the upper curve but both curves appear concave toward the origin]

sourceQuestion:
P, Q, R and S are four types of dangerous microbes recently found in a human habitat. The area of each circle with its diameter printed in brackets represents the growth of a single microbe surviving human immunity system within 24 hours of entering the body. The danger to human beings varies proportionately with the toxicity, potency and growth attributed to a microbe shown in the figure below.

[Bubble chart: x-axis = Potency (0-10), y-axis = Toxicity (0-6). Circles: P(1.2) at potency~4, toxicity~5; Q(2) at potency~2, toxicity~2; R(0.5) at potency~7, toxicity~1; S(4) at potency~5, toxicity~4]

A pharmaceutical company is contemplating the development of a vaccine against the most dangerous microbe. Which microbe should be the target in its first attempt?

A. P     B. Q     C. R     D. S

parsed.stem:
P, Q, R and S are four types of dangerous microbes recently found in a human habitat. The area of each circle with its diameter printed in brackets represents the growth of a single microbe surviving human immunity system within 24 hours of entering the body. The danger to human beings varies proportionately with the toxicity, potency and growth attributed to a microbe shown in the figure below.

[Bubble chart: x-axis = Potency (0-10), y-axis = Toxicity (0-6). Circles: P(1.2) at potency~4, toxicity~5; Q(2) at potency~2, toxicity~2; R(0.5) at potency~7, toxicity~1; S(4) at potency~5, toxicity~4]

A pharmaceutical company is contemplating the development of a vaccine against the most dangerous microbe. Which microbe should be the target in its first attempt?

sourceQuestion:
Weight of a person can be expressed as a function of their age. The function usually varies from person to person. Suppose this function is identical for two brothers, and it monotonically increases till the age of 50 years and then it monotonically decreases. Let a_1 and a_2 (in years) denote the ages of the brothers and a_1 < a_2.

Which one of the following statements is correct about the age when they attain the same weight?

A. a_1 <= 50 <= a_2
B. a_1 < 50 < a_2
C. 30 < a_1 < a_2
D. Either a_1 = 50 or a_2 = 50

parsed.stem:
Weight of a person can be expressed as a function of their age. The function usually varies from person to person. Suppose this function is identical for two brothers, and it monotonically increases till the age of 50 years and then it monotonically decreases. Let a_1 and a_2 (in years) denote the ages of the brothers and a_1 < a_2.

Which one of the following statements is correct about the age when they attain the same weight?

sourceQuestion:
The temperature T in a room varies as a function of the outside temperature T_0 and the number of persons in the room p, according to the relation T = K(T_0)(p^theta), where K and theta are constants.

What would be the value of theta given the following data?

| T_0 | p | T    |
|-----|---|------|
| 27  | 2 | 30.0 |
| 30  | 5 | 42.0 |

A. 0.8   B. 1.0   C. 2.0   D. 10.0

parsed.stem:
The temperature T in a room varies as a function of the outside temperature T_0 and the number of persons in the room p, according to the relation T = K(T_0)(p^theta), where K and theta are constants.

What would be the value of theta given the following data?

| T_0 | p | T    |
|-----|---|------|
| 27  | 2 | 30.0 |
| 30  | 5 | 42.0 |

sourceQuestion:
A cab was involved in a hit and run accident at night. You are given the following data about the cabs in the city and the accident.

i.   85% of cabs in the city are green and the remaining cabs are blue.
ii.  A witness identified the cab involved in the accident as blue.
iii. It is known that a witness can correctly identify the cab colour only 80% of the time.

Which of the following options is closest to the probability that the accident was caused by a blue cab?

A. 12%   B. 15%   C. 41%   D. 80%

parsed.stem:
A cab was involved in a hit and run accident at night. You are given the following data about the cabs in the city and the accident.

i.   85% of cabs in the city are green and the remaining cabs are blue.
ii.  A witness identified the cab involved in the accident as blue.
iii. It is known that a witness can correctly identify the cab colour only 80% of the time.

Which of the following options is closest to the probability that the accident was caused by a blue cab?

sourceQuestion:
Five people P, Q, R, S and T work in a bank. P and Q don't like each other but have to share an office till T gets a promotion and moves to the big office next to the garden. R, who is currently sharing an office with T, wants to move to the adjacent office with S, the handsome new intern. Given the floor plan below, what is the current location of Q, R and T?

Floor Plan (O = Office, WI = Washroom):
[Garden side: Big Office | Office A | Office B]
[Entrance side: Office C | WI | Office D]

A. Q: Office C, R: Office A, T: Office A
B. Q: Office A, R: Office D, T: Office D
C. Q: Office C, R: Office D, T: Office D
D. Q: Office D, R: Office A, T: Office A

(O=Office, WI=Washroom)
sourceAnswer: C. Q: Office C, R: Office D, T: Office D

parsed.stem:
Five people P, Q, R, S and T work in a bank. P and Q don't like each other but have to share an office till T gets a promotion and moves to the big office next to the garden. R, who is currently sharing an office with T, wants to move to the adjacent office with S, the handsome new intern. Given the floor plan below, what is the current location of Q, R and T?

Floor Plan (O = Office, WI = Washroom):
[Garden side: Big Office | Office A | Office B]
[Entrance side: Office C | WI | Office D]

options.text: Q: Office C, R: Office A, T: Office A
Q: Office A, R: Office D, T: Office D
Q: Office C, R: Office D, T: Office D
Q: Office D, R: Office A, T: Office A (O=Office, WI=Washroom)

sourceQuestion:
A 4 x 4 digital image has pixel intensities (U) as shown in the figure. The number of pixels with U <= 4 is:

Pixel intensity matrix:
0  1  1  0
2  4  7  3
3  5  4  5
6  7  3  2

A. 3    B. 8    C. 11    D. 9

Pixel intensity matrix:
0  1  1  0
2  4  7  3
3  5  4  5
6  7  3  2

sourceQuestion:
Out of all the 2-digit integers between 1 and 100, a 2-digit number has to be selected at random. What is the probability that the selected number is not divisible by 2?

A. 13/90    B. 12/90    C. 78/90    D. 9/10

parsed.stem: Out of all the 2-digit integers between 1 and 100, a 2-digit number has to be selected at random. What is the probability that the selected number is not divisible by 2?

sourceQuestion:
What would be the smallest natural number which when divided either by 20 or by 42 or by 76 leaves a remainder of 7 in each case?

A. 3047    B. 6047    C. 7987    D. 63847

parsed.stem: What would be the smallest natural number which when divided either by 20 or by 42 or by 76 leaves a remainder of 7 in each case?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the set of integers {1, 2, 3, ..., 5000}. The number of integers that is divisible by neither 3 nor 4 is:

A. 1668  B. 2084  C. 2500  D. 2016

parsed.stem: Consider the set of integers {1, 2, 3, ..., 5000}. The number of integers that is divisible by neither 3 nor 4 is:

sourceQuestion:
If the number 715(d)423 is divisible by 3 (where d denotes the missing digit), then the smallest whole number value of d is:

A. 0  B. 2  C. 5  D. 6

parsed.stem: If the number 715(d)423 is divisible by 3 (where d denotes the missing digit), then the smallest whole number value of d is:

sourceQuestion:
In appreciation of the social improvements completed in a town, a wealthy philanthropist decided to gift Rs 750 to each male senior citizen in the town and Rs 1000 to each female senior citizen. Altogether, there were 300 senior citizens eligible for this gift. However, only 1/2 of the eligible men and 3/4 of the eligible women claimed the gift. How much money (in Rupees) did the philanthropist give away in total?

A. 1,50,000  B. 2,00,000  C. 1,75,000  D. 1,51,000

parsed.stem: In appreciation of the social improvements completed in a town, a wealthy philanthropist decided to gift Rs 750 to each male senior citizen in the town and Rs 1000 to each female senior citizen. Altogether, there were 300 senior citizens eligible for this gift. However, only 1/2 of the eligible men and 3/4 of the eligible women claimed the gift. How much money (in Rupees) did the philanthropist give away in total?

sourceQuestion:
Both the numerator and the denominator of 3/17 are increased by a positive integer z, and those of 5/19 are decreased by the same integer z. This operation results in the same value for both the fractions. What is the value of z?

A. 1  B. 2  C. 3  D. 4

parsed.stem: Both the numerator and the denominator of 3/17 are increased by a positive integer z, and those of 5/19 are decreased by the same integer z. This operation results in the same value for both the fractions. What is the value of z?

sourceQuestion:
For positive integers p and q, with p/q != 1, if (p/q)^(p/q) = p^(1/q), then which of the following must be TRUE?

A. p^(p-1) = q^p  B. p^q = q^p  C. sqrt(q) = sqrt(p)  D. sqrt(q) = p

parsed.stem: For positive integers p and q, with p/q != 1, if (p/q)^(p/q) = p^(1/q), then which of the following must be TRUE?

sourceQuestion:
If q^(-a) = 1/r and r^(-b) = 1/s and s^(-c) = 1/q, the value of abc is:

A. (rqs)^(-1)  B. 0  C. 1  D. r + q + s

parsed.stem: If q^(-a) = 1/r and r^(-b) = 1/s and s^(-c) = 1/q, the value of abc is:

sourceQuestion:
If p, q, r, s are distinct integers such that:
f(p, q, r, s) = max(p, q, r, s)
g(p, q, r, s) = min(p, q, r, s)
h(p, q, r, s) = remainder of (p * q) / (r * s) if (p * q) > (r * s)
or remainder of (r * s) / (p * q) if (r * s) > (p * q)

Also a function m(p, q) = f(p, q) = max(p, q) or g(p, q) = min(p, q) depending on the two-variable form.

Also the same operations are valid with two variable functions of the form f(p, q).

What is the value of f(f(3,5), g(1,3), g(2,-1), h(5,6)) where the operations combine as f(max(3,5), min(1,3), min(2,-1), h(5,6))?

Actually, what is f(f(3,5,7,3), g(1,3,7,2), g(3,2,-1,5), h(5,6,2,1))?

A. 59  B. 45  C. 43  D. 35

parsed.stem:
If p, q, r, s are distinct integers such that:
f(p, q, r, s) = max(p, q, r, s)
g(p, q, r, s) = min(p, q, r, s)
h(p, q, r, s) = remainder of (p * q) / (r * s) if (p * q) > (r * s)
or remainder of (r * s) / (p * q) if (r * s) > (p * q)

Also a function m(p, q) = f(p, q) = max(p, q) or g(p, q) = min(p, q) depending on the two-variable form.

Also the same operations are valid with two variable functions of the form f(p, q).

What is the value of f(f(3,5), g(1,3), g(2,-1), h(5,6)) where the operations combine as f(max(3,5), min(1,3), min(2,-1), h(5,6))?

Actually, what is f(f(3,5,7,3), g(1,3,7,2), g(3,2,-1,5), h(5,6,2,1))?

sourceQuestion:
A function f(x) is linear and has a value of 29 at x = -2 and 39 at x = 3. Find its value at x = 5.

A. 59  B. 45  C. 43  D. 35

parsed.stem: A function f(x) is linear and has a value of 29 at x = -2 and 39 at x = 3. Find its value at x = 5.

sourceQuestion:
Choose the most appropriate equation for the function drawn as thick line, in the plot below.

[Plot shows a piecewise curve: for y < 0 the function z equals twice the negative y value (V-shape reflected), for y >= 0 the function z is 0]

A. z = y - |y|  B. z = -(y - |y|)  C. z = y + |y|  D. z = -(y + |y|)

parsed.stem:
Choose the most appropriate equation for the function drawn as thick line, in the plot below.

[Plot shows a piecewise curve: for y < 0 the function z equals twice the negative y value (V-shape reflected), for y >= 0 the function z is 0]

sourceQuestion:
Consider two functions of time t:
f(t) = 0.01t^2
g(t) = 4t
where 0 < t < infinity.

Now consider the following two statements:
i. For some t > 0, g(t) > f(t).
ii. There exists a T such that f(t) > g(t) for all t > T.

Which one of the following options is TRUE?

A. only (i) is correct
B. only (ii) is correct
C. both (i) and (ii) are correct
D. neither (i) nor (ii) is correct

parsed.stem:
Consider two functions of time t:
f(t) = 0.01t^2
g(t) = 4t
where 0 < t < infinity.

Now consider the following two statements:
i. For some t > 0, g(t) > f(t).
ii. There exists a T such that f(t) > g(t) for all t > T.

Which one of the following options is TRUE?

sourceQuestion:
f(x) and g(y) are functions of x and y, respectively, and f(x) - g(y) = 0 for all values of x and y. Which one of the following options is necessarily TRUE for x and y?

A. f(x) = 0 and g(y) = 0
B. f(x) = g(y) = constant
C. f(x) if constant and g(y) if constant
D. f(x) + g(y) = f(x) * g(y)

parsed.stem: f(x) and g(y) are functions of x and y, respectively, and f(x) - g(y) = 0 for all values of x and y. Which one of the following options is necessarily TRUE for x and y?

options.text: f(x) if constant and g(y) if constant

sourceQuestion:
If f(x) = x^2 for each x in (-inf, inf), then f(f(f(x))) / f(x) is equal to _______.

A. f(x)
B. (f(x))^2
C. (f(x))^3
D. (f(x))^8

parsed.stem: If f(x) = x^2 for each x in (-inf, inf), then f(f(f(x))) / f(x) is equal to _______.

sourceQuestion:
A function, lambda, is defined by

lambda(p, q) = (p - q)^2,  if p >= 0
               p,           if p < 0

The value of the expression lambda(-3 + 2, -2 + 3) is:

A. -1
B. 0
C. 9/4
D. 16

parsed.stem:
A function, lambda, is defined by

lambda(p, q) = (p - q)^2,  if p >= 0
               p,           if p < 0

The value of the expression lambda(-3 + 2, -2 + 3) is:
